name: Almond-Streusel Peach Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine 1 cup flour and 1/2 teaspoon salt. Add 4 tablespoons chilled butter and shortening. Mix until the mixture resembles coarse meal with a few pea-sized pieces remaining.
2. Gradually sprinkle in about 3 to 4 tablespoons ice water, one tablespoon at a time, until the dough just comes together. Gather into a disk, wrap, and chill for at least 30 minutes.
3. In another bowl, combine the remaining 1 cup flour, 1/3 cup brown sugar, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg. Add the remaining 6 tablespoons chilled butter and mix until crumbly. Stir in the sliced almonds. Chill.
4. Preheat the oven to 475°F (245°C).
5. Roll out the chilled pie pastry on a lightly floured work surface to fit a 9-inch pie pan. Transfer the pastry into the pan, trim and flute the edges, then chill.
6. Grate 1 teaspoon lemon zest from the lemon and squeeze about 3 tablespoons of juice into a bowl. Peel and slice the peaches, then combine with the remaining 2/3 cup brown sugar, 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg, lemon zest and juice, cornstarch, and almond extract.
7. Sprinkle bread crumbs over the bottom of the pie shell and pour in the peach mixture. Sprinkle the almond crumb mixture on top.
8. Bake at 475°F (245°C) for 15 minutes. Reduce the temperature to 350°F (175°C) and continue baking until the top is browned and the fruit juices are bubbling, about 50 to 55 minutes. Allow to cool completely before cutting.
